comments.body: Thank you! I was getting some build errors when generating dokka for unit tests, your suggestion of creating a separate task worked!
Possibly an out of scope question but how did you manage to ship the html docs with your SDK lib?
@alizeyn We ship the documentation as a separate archive. Together with our integration guide and other documentation. All of this is forwarded to the integrators.

answers.body:
Dokka doesn't automatically create a source set for androidTest, and custom DokkaTasks require explicit configuration to change their output format from the default (HTML).

In your library module's build.gradle file, add the following configuration. This assumes you are using the Groovy DSL.

import org.jetbrains.dokka.gradle.DokkaTask
import org.jetbrains.dokka.base.DokkaBase

apply plugin: 'org.jetbrains.dokka'


// 1. Configure the default task for the main library Javadoc
tasks.named('dokkaJavadoc', DokkaTask) {
    outputDirectory.set(file("$buildDir/docs/javadoc/library"))
    dokkaSourceSets {
        named("main") {
            includes.from(files("dokka_modules_main.md")) 
        }
    }
}

// 2. Register a new, separate task for the androidTest Javadoc
tasks.register('dokkaJavadocTest', DokkaTask) {
    description = "Generates Javadoc for the Android test source set."
    
    pluginConfiguration(DokkaBase) {
        format = "javadoc"
    }

    outputDirectory.set(file("$buildDir/docs/javadoc/androidTest"))

    dokkaSourceSets {
        create("androidTest") {
            def variant = android.libraryVariants.find { it.name == 'debug' }
            if (variant == null) {
                throw new GradleException("Cannot find 'debug' variant to configure Dokka for tests.")
            }


            sourceRoots.from(android.sourceSets.main.java.srcDirs)
            sourceRoots.from(android.sourceSets.androidTest.java.srcDirs)
            
            // sourceRoots.from(android.sourceSets.main.kotlin.srcDirs)
            // sourceRoots.from(android.sourceSets.androidTest.kotlin.srcDirs)

            classpath.from(variant.getAndroidTestCompileClasspath())
            classpath.from(variant.javaCompileProvider.get().destinationDir)

            includes.from(files("dokka_modules_test.md")) 
            noAndroidSdkLink.set(false)
            includeNonPublic.set(false)
            skipEmptyPackages.set(true)
            reportUndocumented.set(false) 
            skipDeprecated.set(false)
        }
    }
}

title: How can I generate Javadoc for my Android library with Dokka for both the main and the androidTest source sets?
body:
I am slowly moving my library project from Java to Kotlin. I have a requirement that I also need to provide Javadoc for both the library and the Android instrumentation tests in has. I can't use Javadoc anymore as it dies when it sees a *.kt file. So I turned to Dokka.

My issue is that I can easily generate Javadoc with Dokka, but only for the main source set. But I can't get it to generate docus for my instrumentation tests.

I have the following configuration in the library build gradle:

apply plugin: 'org.jetbrains.dokka'
...
dokkaJavadoc.configure {
    dokkaSourceSets {
        named("main") {
            // Main source set conf
            outputDirectory = file("dokkadoc/service-doc")
            includes.from(files("dokka_modules_test.md"))
            sourceRoots.from(file("src/main/java"))
        }
        named("androidTest") {
            // Test source set conf
            outputDirectory = file("dokkadoc/service-test-doc")
            includes.from(files("dokka_modules_test.md"))
            sourceRoots.from(file("src/androidTest/java"))
        }
        configureEach {
            // Shared conf for all source sets
            noAndroidSdkLink.set(false)
            includeNonPublic.set(false)
            skipEmptyPackages.set(true)
            reportUndocumented.set(true)
            skipDeprecated.set(false)
        }

    }
}

In the root build.grade I import:

buildscript {
    ..
    ext.dokka_version = '1.4.10.2'
    repositories {
        ..
    }
    dependencies {
        classpath "org.jetbrains.dokka:dokka-gradle-plugin:$dokka_version"
    }
}

What am I doing wrong? Or did I misunderstood and I can't generate sepatate Javadocs this way? Do I have to generate extension tasks for all build variants, a la:

libraryVariants.all { variant ->

..

def variantDokkadoc = task("dokkadoc${variant.name.capitalize()}", type: org.jetbrains.dokka.gradle.DokkaTask) {
...
}

EDIT: Seems when I create a separate task for the tests part like this then it does it:

// Dokka task for Service tests
task dokkaJavadocTests(type: org.jetbrains.dokka.gradle.DokkaTask) {
    dokkaSourceSets {
        named("main") {
            outputDirectory.set(file("dokkadoc/service-test-doc"))
            includes.from(files("dokka_modules_test.md"))
            sourceRoots.setFrom(file("src/main/java"))
            sourceRoots.from(file("src/androidTest/java"))

            noAndroidSdkLink.set(false)
            includeNonPublic.set(false)
            skipEmptyPackages.set(true)
            reportUndocumented.set(true)
            skipDeprecated.set(false)
        }
    }
}

For some reason, I have to go with

named("main")

otherwise it ignores the test classes.

Also, I am still unable to generate the Javadoc version, it generates the HTML one. If I add

outputFormat = "javadoc"

then it just ignores it

EDIT 2:

For now, I solved this by making a second task for tests and keeping both of them in HTML format. Eg:

// Main dokka task for the library
dokkaHtml.configure {
    dokkaSourceSets {
        named("main") {
            // Main source set conf
            outputDirectory.set(file("dokkadoc/${project.ext.versionNameDokkaDoc}"))
            includes.from(files("dokka_modules_main.md"))

            noAndroidSdkLink.set(false)
            includeNonPublic.set(false)
            skipEmptyPackages.set(true)
            reportUndocumented.set(true)
            skipDeprecated.set(false)
        }
    }
}

// Dokka task for the library tests
task dokkaHtmlTests(type: org.jetbrains.dokka.gradle.DokkaTask) {
    dokkaSourceSets {
        named("main") {
            outputDirectory.set(file("dokkadoc/${project.ext.versionNameDokkaDocTests}"))
            includes.from(files("dokka_modules_test.md"))
            sourceRoots.setFrom(file("src/main/java"))
            sourceRoots.from(file("src/androidTest/java"))

            noAndroidSdkLink.set(false)
            includeNonPublic.set(false)
            skipEmptyPackages.set(true)
            reportUndocumented.set(true)
            skipDeprecated.set(false)
        }
    }
}
